findings.claim: ADR 012 (Accepted) requires a single Kubernetes cluster hosting dev, staging and production workloads, partitioned only by namespace, while ADR 036 (Accepted) requires splitting workloads across multiple clusters by environment type (production vs non-production). A codebase cannot simultaneously run all environments in one cluster and run production/non-production in separate clusters.

citations.quote: After consideration of the pros and cons of each approach we went with one cluster, using namespaces to partition different workloads.
start adopting multi-cluster by splitting out workloads by _environment type_, i.e. _production_ and _non-production_

findings.consequence: Infrastructure-as-code and namespace provisioning tooling cannot honor both records at once: one mandates a single cluster topology, the other mandates a split-by-environment topology. A maintainer relying on ADR 012 as the live decision would build against reality that ADR 036 has already overridden, and vice versa.

findings.claim: ADR 014 (Accepted) requires the team to self-manage the Kubernetes control plane (kOps) rather than use a managed AWS offering, while ADR 022 (Accepted) requires using the managed Amazon EKS control plane in place of kOps for the same main cluster. These are mutually exclusive control-plane management models.

citations.quote: We decided to manage the kubernetes cluster ourselves rather than using EKS mainly for the below reasons:
Use Amazon EKS for running the main cluster, which hosts MOJ service teams' applications. This replaces usage of kOps.

findings.consequence: A team following ADR 014 as still-live guidance would continue operating a self-managed kOps control plane, while ADR 022 explicitly declares that approach replaced by EKS. Since ADR 014 carries no Superseded marker, the log presents both as current, which will mislead anyone auditing control-plane ownership.

findings.claim: ADR 003 (Accepted) requires deployment pipelines to run on a self-hosted Concourse CI system, while ADR 040 (Accepted) requires switching deployment pipelines to GitHub Actions and decommissioning Concourse. The two records specify different, incompatible CI/CD systems as the required pipeline technology.

citations.quote: Replace self hosted Jenkins with self hosted Concourse CI pipeline
We will switch to using GitHub Actions for our deployment pipelines.

findings.consequence: Teams consulting the log for the current CI/CD standard get two Accepted answers pointing at different tools; without ADR 003 being marked superseded, someone could stand up new Concourse-based pipelines in the belief that ADR 003 still governs, directly contradicting the GitHub Actions mandate in ADR 040.
